title: Xtra Special British Monkfish Yorkshire Pudding
description: A salty, springy, and cozy British dish featuring monkfish, this recipe is a twist on the classic Yorkshire pudding, with a diabetic-friendly spin.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, eggs, milk, salt, and pepper.
Add the monkfish pieces to the bowl and stir to coat with the batter.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the namul to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, until lightly browned.
Pour the batter into a greased 12-cup Yorkshire pudding pan.
Add the cooked namul and monkfish to the batter and stir gently.
Bake the Yorkshire pudding for 20-25 minutes, until puffed and golden brown.
Remove the pudding from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
